task: I’m looking at two used cars this weekend and want to keep my notes organized while I’m at the lot. Can you help me think through a straightforward way to compare the main facts so I don’t miss anything important while I’m checking them out?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — I’d keep it simple and use the same short template for both cars so you can compare them side by side.

### Quick compare sheet
For each car, jot down:

- **Year / make / model / trim**
- **Price**
- **Mileage**
- **VIN**
- **Title status / history**
- **Accident or damage notes**
- **Number of owners**
- **Service records available?**
- **Tire condition**
- **Brake feel / any weird noises**
- **Warning lights on?**
- **Leaks / smells / rust**
- **A/C and heat work?**
- **Test drive notes**
- **Anything that needs fixing soon**

### Easy scoring idea
If you want a fast way to compare them, mark each item as:

- **Good**
- **Okay**
- **Concern**

That keeps you from overthinking it at the lot.

### Two things I’d especially not skip
- **Tires and brakes**
- **Warning lights / fresh leaks**

Those tend to be the easiest “big problem” clues to spot quickly.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page fill-in checklist** you can copy into your notes app.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right idea. But I want it a little more disciplined than just a loose list.

Can you make it into a tighter side-by-side checklist with columns for **Car A / Car B / notes / concern level**? I’d want the main facts up top first — year, mileage, price, title status, accidents, service history — then the mechanical stuff I can check fast at the lot.

Also add a spot for:
- cold start behavior
- steering pull / brake vibration on the test drive
- tire date codes if I can see them
- battery age if listed
- any signs of flood or rust damage

Keep it short enough to use on my phone. I don’t need a long explanation, just something I can actually fill out while I’m standing there.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter phone-friendly version you can copy into Notes.

## Used Car Side-by-Side Checklist

| Item | Car A | Car B | Notes | Concern |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Year / Make / Model / Trim |  |  |  |  |
| Price |  |  |  |  |
| Mileage |  |  |  |  |
| VIN |  |  |  |  |
| Title status |  |  |  |  |
| Accidents / damage |  |  |  |  |
| Service history |  |  |  |  |
| Owners |  |  |  |  |
| Battery age / listed date |  |  |  |  |

### Fast lot checks
| Item | Car A | Car B | Notes | Concern |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cold start behavior |  |  |  |  |
| Warning lights |  |  |  |  |
| Leaks / smells |  |  |  |  |
| Rust / flood signs |  |  |  |  |
| Tire condition |  |  |  |  |
| Tire date codes |  |  |  |  |
| Brake feel / vibration |  |  |  |  |
| Steering pull |  |  |  |  |
| A/C and heat |  |  |  |  |

### Simple concern level
- **Low**
- **Medium**
- **High**

If you want, I can also make this into an even shorter **copy-paste phone version** with one line per item.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Tight enough to use on the lot, and the order makes sense.

I can copy that into Notes and actually use it without scrolling around too much. The extra spots for cold start, tire date codes, and flood/rust signs are the right adds.

No changes needed.
